perturbation.original_text: Section 6. Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party. All obligations of confidentiality shall survive such termination. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient shall promptly return all of Owner's Confidential Information provided to it in tangible form, together with any and all copies and/or summaries, and shall destroy all of Owner's Confidential Information that is electronically stored; provided, however, that each Party's legal department may retain one copy of the Confidential Information in its file solely for the purpose of identifying information designated as "Confidential Information."
perturbation.changed_text: Section 6. Termination. This Agreement shall automatically renew every year unless either Party provides a notice of non-renewal 30 days before the anniversary of the Effective Date. The confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 2 shall terminate immediately upon the termination of this agreement for any reason. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient is not required to return or destroy any Confidential Information, but must cease using it.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that either party may terminate at any time and that all confidentiality obligations survive termination. This change introduces the concept of automatic renewal unless there's a notice of non-renewal, and it stipulates that confidentiality obligations terminate upon agreement termination. It also eliminates the requirement to return or destroy confidential information, only requiring cessation of use. This directly contradicts the original text, and it especially impacts sections that rely on perpetual confidentiality, creating uncertainty and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not use Owner's Confidential Information except for purposes of evaluating, maintaining and furthering a business relationship with Owner. Recipient shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information received from Owner and shall not directly or indirectly disclose such information to any person or entity except Recipient's employees and consultants, and then only on a need-to-know basis. Recipient shall ensure that such employees and consultants are bound by a written agreement to protect the received Confidential Information from unauthorized use and disclosure. Recipient shall protect Owner's Confidential Information from disclosure to others using the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own most highly confidential information, but in no event less than a reasonable standard of care. Recipient shall not make or distribute any more copies or summaries of Owner's Confidential Information than are necessary to evaluate, maintain and further a business relationship between the Parties, and shall ensure that all such copies or summaries are marked as confidential and the property of Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both mandate that Confidential Information is maintained in confidence. The changed text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations terminate upon agreement termination. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted section mandates ongoing confidentiality, while the changed text allows for termination of confidentiality, which contradicts the contradicted section's obligations to maintain confidentiality. This aligns with the Inconsistencies definition for In-Text Contradictions where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that Owner will have no adequate remedy at law if Recipient does not comply with its obligations under this Agreement. Therefore, Owner shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have, to obtain in any court of competent jurisdiction temporary, preliminary and permanent injunctive relief to restrain any breach, threatened breach, or otherwise to specifically enforce any obligations of Recipient if Recipient fails to perform any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that monetary damages are sufficient to compensate Owner for any breach of this Agreement. Therefore, Owner's sole remedy for any breach of this Agreement shall be limited to monetary damages not exceeding $1,000. Injunctive relief is explicitly waived by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants the right to injunctive relief. This modification explicitly waives injunctive relief and limits remedies to a fixed monetary amount. This directly contradicts the premise of Section 7, that injunctive relief is available, and creates an in-text contradiction as it is inconsistent with the idea that Confidential Information is so unique that monetary damages would not be sufficient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties acknowledge that Confidential Information is unique and valuable, and that Owner will have no adequate remedy at law if Recipient does not comply with its obligations under this Agreement. Therefore, Owner shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have, to obtain in any court of competent jurisdiction temporary, preliminary and permanent injunctive relief to restrain any breach, threatened breach, or otherwise to specifically enforce any obligations of Recipient if Recipient fails to perform any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly waives injunctive relief and limits remedies to a fixed monetary amount. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Owner shall have the right to obtain injunctive relief, creating a clear and direct conflict and inconsistency within the document regarding available remedies. The explanation provided further confirms this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the internal laws of the State of Washington, and, where such laws are preempted by the laws of the United States, by the internal laws of the United States, in each case without regard to conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, including its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies Washington law, while the revised clause specifies Delaware law, including its conflict of laws principles. This introduces a fundamental conflict regarding which state's laws govern the agreement and its interpretation. This has a direct impact on Section 8, specifically, any judgement made will have to be arbitrated through the correct state's judgements, but now that is contradicted by this text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Arbitration. In the event of any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach or interpretation thereof, the parties shall, upon five days notice from either one to the other, submit themselves and the subject-matter of the dispute to arbitration in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association by a single, disinterested arbitrator appointed in accordance with such Rules. The determination of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding. Judgment upon the award rendered may be entered in any court of any state or country having jurisdiction. Each party shall ensure that any arbitration is conducted as speedily as is reasonably possible, and that all and any information disclosed during or in connection with the arbitration is treated by each party with the strictest confidence. Any arbitration conducted under or in connection with this Agreement shall take place in Seattle, Washington at a time and location to be determined by the arbitrator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Washington to Delaware, including its conflict of laws principles. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that any arbitration conducted under the agreement shall take place in Seattle, Washington. The change in governing law introduces a fundamental conflict regarding the location and legal framework for resolving disputes, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the perturbation explanation. This satisfies the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice or other communication under this Agreement given by either party to the other party shall be deemed to be properly given if given in writing and delivered (i) by nationally recognized private courier (e.g., Federal Express) or (ii) by mail (return receipt requested), properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the recipient at the address identified in its signature block to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice or other communication under this Agreement may be given by email to the designated email addresses of the parties. If no email address is explicitly provided in the signature block, using the standard business email format (firstname.lastname@companydomain.com) will be sufficient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires written notice delivered by courier or mail. The revision allows notice by email, and even suggests a default email format if none is provided. This conflicts directly with the original requirement of physical delivery and the need for return receipt, potentially invalidating any notices sent via email under the original terms. This contradiction affects all other sections, especially Section 6, as any termination notice has to be delivered in the way stated in Section 8.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party. All obligations of confidentiality shall survive such termination. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient shall promptly return all of Owner's Confidential Information provided to it in tangible form, together with any and all copies and/or summaries, and shall destroy all of Owner's Confidential Information that is electronically stored; provided, however, that each Party's legal department may retain one copy of the Confidential Information in its file solely for the purpose of identifying information designated as "Confidential Information."
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates specific delivery methods (courier or mail with return receipt) for notices. The changed text introduces email as an acceptable method, even suggesting a default email format. This directly contradicts the requirement for physical delivery and return receipt, which is important to the contradicted text regarding termination notices, creating an inconsistency regarding how termination notices should be delivered.
